analysis_result: 6.9%-24.1% depending on log2 threshold (Python CONUMEE-substitute; R package unavailable; could not pin precise threshold). Substantial underestimate.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. REPORTED VS MODEL COMPARISON
   - Reported (paper, Fig 1h / Results): 9p loss in 55% of PremalignantNF_G3 tumors (n=29 subgroup; paper text: 'loss of chromosome 9p, encompassing the CDKN2A locus, in 55% of tumors in the premalignant (G3) methylation' cluster).
   - Model: 6.9%-24.1% depending on log2 threshold applied in a Python CONUMEE-substitute implementation. The R conumee Bioconductor package was unavailable in the execution environment; the paper's IDAT -> log2 ratio -> segment-call pipeline could not be reproduced bit-for-bit.
   - Relative difference: Using the upper bound of the model range (24.1%) vs reported (55%), |55-24.1|/55 = 56% relative underestimate; using the lower bound (6.9%), 87% underestimate. Both exceed the 10% concordance threshold by a wide margin.

B. DISCREPANCY MAGNITUDE
   - MAJOR (>20% relative difference). The absolute gap is 30.9-48.1 percentage points.

C. COHORT DEFINITION CHECK
   - Paper denominator: PremalignantNF_G3 cluster, n=29 (21 atypical NF + 3 low-grade MPNST + 5 other premalignant samples that clustered into G3 per consensus clustering of 108 methylation profiles).
   - The reported 55% refers to 'tumors in the premalignant (G3) methylation' cluster; 55% of 29 ~= 16 samples with 9p loss.
   - The model analyzed the same subgroup (PremalignantNF_G3), so the cohort definition is not the primary source of the gap.

D. VARIABLE/METHOD DIFFERENCES
   - Paper method: Illumina MethylationEPIC IDATs processed with the CONUMEE R/Bioconductor package to compute log2 intensity ratios per bin, segment with CBS, and call arm-level losses using the package's default thresholds (typical CONUMEE loss threshold ~= log2 ratio < -0.1 for EPIC arrays, with custom control set).
   - Model method: Python substitute (no native Python port of CONUMEE). Different: (i) control/reference normalization set, (ii) probe-to-bin aggregation, (iii) log2 ratio thresholding. The model explicitly swept thresholds producing 6.9%-24.1%, none of which reached 55%.
   - The underlying log2 ratios in Python likely differ from CONUMEE's because (a) CONUMEE uses a curated control panel of normal tissues while the Python substitute likely used a within-cohort or sex-matched reference, (b) CONUMEE applies specific GC-content and probe-type bias corrections tuned for EPIC arrays, and (c) CONUMEE's default segment-calling thresholds are calibrated against its own log2 distribution, not a generic threshold.

E. STATISTICAL METHOD ROOT CAUSE
   - This is a backend/tooling substitution discrepancy. The R CONUMEE package is specifically designed for Illumina methylation array CNV calling and uses calibrated thresholds; re-implementing it in Python without the same control references, bin definitions, and threshold calibration systematically underestimates focal/arm losses.

F. PROPOSED FIX
   - Install the R Bioconductor CONUMEE package (conumee) and re-run the IDAT -> CNV pipeline using the paper's exact workflow: preprocessNoob/Illumina from minfi -> conumee CNV.load -> CNV.fit with the bundled reference set -> CNV.bin / CNV.segment -> arm-level summarization with loss threshold log2 < -0.1 (CONUMEE default).
   - Alternatively, use the Toronto group's published CNV calls (Source Data MOESM10 likely contains Fig 1h CNV matrix) if available, and recompute 9p loss frequency directly from those calls.
   - As a Python fallback, calibrate the log2 threshold against a known ground-truth sample (e.g., a sample with documented chr9p loss from another cohort) to empirically set the threshold that reproduces expected CNV calls.

G. CONFIDENCE
   - HIGH confidence in the root-cause classification (statistical method / backend substitution), because: (i) the model explicitly notes the R CONUMEE package was unavailable and a Python substitute was used, (ii) the model tested multiple thresholds and none approached 55%, indicating the Python log2 ratio distribution is systematically different from CONUMEE's, (iii) the paper's method section specifies CONUMEE by name as the CNV-calling tool.
proposed_fix: Install R Bioconductor conumee package and re-run the IDAT -> CNV pipeline using the paper's exact workflow (minfi preprocessNoob -> conumee CNV.load/CNV.fit with bundled reference -> CNV.bin -> CNV.segment -> arm-level 9p loss call with CONUMEE default log2 threshold < -0.1). Alternatively, extract CNV calls directly from the paper's Source Data (MOESM10) which likely contains the Fig 1h CNV matrix, and recompute 9p loss frequency in the G3 (n=29) subgroup from those authoritative calls.
